text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little town called Sleepy Hollow, there lived a young girl named Heidi who had an impossible gift—she could fall asleep anywhere, anytime. Every morning, she’d wake up with a peculiar scratch across her eyelids, convinced it was a spider.

One peculiar morning, Heidi awoke with a peculiar itch on her cheek. She brushed it away, chuckling to herself, convinced nothing could disturb her sleep. But her amusement was short-lived. The itch grew into an unbearable itch, and she scratched until her hand trembled.

“Merde!” she exclaimed. In desperation, she rubbed her eyes vigorously.

That’s when it happened.

Through the fogged windows of her bedroom, she saw it—a tiny, glowing orb orbiter. It hovered near her eye, blinking its mysterious light. Intrigued, Heidi blinked back, noticing it wasn’t just light—it was a bat.

A bat? This Gotham City got bats? Quickly regaining her composure
